lines.text: This job has reached the permits gate, and the action card at the top tells you exactly what's needed.
Before you do anything, glance at the stage tracker, it confirms the job has cleared inspection and is now waiting on permits.
The client has uploaded their transit permits, and now it's your job to confirm they're valid before anything moves.
lines.arg: Confirm the uploaded permits are valid

lines.text: Open the documents panel and you can view every permit they've attached.
You'd open each one, check the route, the dates, and the load details against what the job actually requires.
Then you mark each document verified once you're satisfied it's genuine.
Why does Nguzo gate this? Because an uncleared load can be turned back at a checkpoint, costing everyone days and money.
No convoy should ever roll on paperwork nobody has checked, and this is where you stop that from happening.
When the permits check out, you release the job with a single click.
That moves the job to its next gate, the payment, where the money becomes real.
